name: Castillo de Jadraque — trailrunning circular desde Jadraque
region_intro: Jogging around Alcarria Alta offers diverse terrain across undulating plateaus and river valleys, primarily located in Guadalajara Province, Spain. The region features limestone and gypsum formations, with rivers like the Cifuentes and Tagus carving deep valleys and gorges. Runners can experience a mix of landscapes, from pine forests to agricultural fields, providing varied routes for different preferences. This natural region is characterized by its smoothly rolling topography, offering both challenging ascents and gentler paths. Best jogging routes around Alcarria Alta The most popular jogging route is Jardín de La Alcarria — circular trailrunning through the lavender fields of Brihuega, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views through lavender fields near Brihuega. Another top favourite among local runners is Castillo de Jadraque — trailrunning circular desde Jadraque, a moderate 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This route provides views of the historic Castillo de Jadraque. Local runners also love the Circular trailrunning through the Peñascos de los Frailes from Las Inviernas, a 7.4 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through unique rock formations, often completed in about 1 hour 28 minutes. Jogging around Alcarria Alta is defined by undulating plateaus, river valleys, and varied forest and agricultural land. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Alcarria Alta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from several reviews. More than 60 runners have used komoot to explore Alcarria Alta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many jogging routes are available in Alcarria Alta?
A: Alcarria Alta offers a wide selection of running trails, with over 20 routes documented on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from moderate paths to more challenging runs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Alcarria Alta?
A: The region is characterized by smoothly undulating plateaus and deep river valleys carved by rivers like the Cifuentes and Tagus. You'll encounter a mix of landscapes, including pine forests, agricultural fields, and unique limestone and gypsum formations. This varied topography provides both challenging ascents and gentler paths along riverbanks.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls or significant natural water features?
A: Yes, the region boasts beautiful water features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032328125/civica-and-civica-waterfall-circular-trailrunning-along-the-tajuna-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Civica and Civica waterfall - circular trailrunning along the Tajuña river&lt;/a&gt; route offers scenic views along the Tajuña River and its waterfall. The Cifuentes River is also known for its picturesque waterfalls.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by historical landmarks or cultural sites?
A: Absolutely. Many running routes in Alcarria Alta integrate cultural and historical elements. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032370069/castillo-de-jadraque-trailrunning-circular-desde-jadraque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Jadraque — trailrunning circular desde Jadraque&lt;/a&gt; route provides views of the historic Castillo de Jadraque. Other routes may take you through charming towns with medieval settings, castles, and historic squares, such as those near Brihuega.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Alcarria Alta?
A: The running routes in Alcarria Alta are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic beauty of the landscapes, and the opportunity to explore both natural wonders and historical sites.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in the area?
A: While many routes are moderate, there are options suitable for various fitness levels. For example, routes around towns like Henche are noted as 'quite easy' and around 7-8 km, perfect for a moderate jog. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Cifuentes&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, moderate option.

Q: Are there any challenging or advanced jogging trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Alcarria Alta offers more demanding routes for experienced runners. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032377449/salinas-de-la-inesperada-and-pico-del-alar-from-ocentejo-circular-trailrunning-in-the-alto-tajo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de la Inesperada and Pico del Alar from Ocentejo — circular trailrunning in the Alto Tajo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of the Twelve Spouts loop from Brihuega&lt;/a&gt; are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and longer distances.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Alcarria Alta?
A: Many of the jogging routes in Alcarria Alta are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032318863/jardin-de-la-alcarria-circular-trailrunning-through-the-lavender-fields-of-brihuega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jardín de La Alcarria — circular trailrunning through the lavender fields of Brihuega&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032331605/circular-trailrunning-through-the-penascos-de-los-frailes-from-las-inviernas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular trailrunning through the Peñascos de los Frailes from Las Inviernas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some unique natural landmarks I can see while running?
A: Beyond the river valleys, you can encounter impressive geological formations. The 'Tetas de Viana,' two iconic twin hills, offer stunning panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocks of the Friars&lt;/a&gt; (Peñascos de los Frailes) are another unique natural monument. The Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park also features impressive gorges and a pristine river.

Q: Are there any routes that go through lavender fields?
A: Yes, a particularly scenic option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032318863/jardin-de-la-alcarria-circular-trailrunning-through-the-lavender-fields-of-brihuega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jardín de La Alcarria — circular trailrunning through the lavender fields of Brihuega&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a beautiful experience, especially when the lavender is in bloom.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Alcarria Alta?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not detailed, the varied landscapes of Alcarria Alta make it suitable for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for running, with vibrant flora in spring and beautiful colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are advisable.

Q: Are there any specific natural parks or protected areas with running trails?
A: Yes, the Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park is a protected area featuring impressive gorges and a pristine river, offering trails with significant ecological value. Additionally, some routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032377449/salinas-de-la-inesperada-and-pico-del-alar-from-ocentejo-circular-trailrunning-in-the-alto-tajo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de la Inesperada and Pico del Alar from Ocentejo&lt;/a&gt;, venture into the Alto Tajo Natural Park.
tips: El Cid or Jadraque Castle is a 15th century Spanish fortress located in the municipality of Jadraque (Guadalajara). From its height, it watches over an extensive area of the Henares river plain, with the mountains of northern Guadalajara in the background.
